disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Colour is beginning to recover (white to blue to red/blotchy) with warming; no digit remains fixed white/blue/black
trigger_question_examples: As you warm your hands, are the fingers changing colour back (going pink/red) or staying white/blue?
Is any single finger staying completely white, blue, or turning black?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're going red and blotchy as they warm, no finger is staying white or turning black.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Bilateral symmetric, cold-triggered attack with no fixed ischaemia, no digital ulcer/gangrene
trigger_question_examples: Did both hands go white symmetrically after the cold?
Any open sores or black areas on the fingertips?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both hands went white after the cold, symmetrically, and there are no sores or black areas.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Am I going to lose my fingers? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Guide immediate core rewarming (whole-body warmth, warm - not hot - water) and reassess colour/pain; explain a recovering bilateral symmetric attack is severe cold-triggered finger colour change, not fixed ischaemia, so amputation is not imminent — but give a clear digital-ischaemia safety-net (ED if a digit stays white/blue/numb despite warming or turns black) and arrange urgent GP review for a vasodilator.
